name: Vía Verde de Tortosa a Xerta
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tortosa offers diverse terrain, from the rugged mountains of Els Ports Natural Park to the flat, expansive landscapes of the Ebro Delta. The region is characterized by deep ravines, dense forests of Scots pines, and the winding course of the Ebro River. This geographical contrast provides a wide range of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Tortosa The most popular mountain bike trail is Vía Verde de Tortosa a Xerta, an easy 17.4 miles (28.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 18 minutes to complete, following an old railway line along the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of the Ebro River – Old railway viaduct loop from Tortosa, a moderate 30.6 miles (49.2 km) path offering scenic views of the Ebro River and its historic viaducts. Local mountain bikers also love the Tortosa Municipal Market – Argilers loop from Tortosa, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Mountain biking around Tortosa is defined by rugged mountain trails, flat delta paths, and routes along riverbanks, with options for both challenging ascents and relaxed rides. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to difficult mountain routes. The routes in Tortosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tortosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in the Terres de l'Ebre region around Tortosa?
A: The region offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find rugged, challenging trails with steep slopes and dense forests in &lt;strong&gt;Els Ports Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by ravines and Scots pines. In contrast, the &lt;strong&gt;Ebro Delta Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; provides flat, expansive landscapes ideal for less strenuous cycling, often through unique wetland ecosystems. The Ebro River banks also offer scenic, generally flat routes.

Q: What is the range of difficulty for mountain bike trails around Tortosa?
A: Mountain biking around Tortosa caters to all skill levels. You can find easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946137928/via-verde-de-tortosa-a-xerta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Tortosa a Xerta&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line. There are also numerous moderate trails, and for experienced riders, challenging routes with significant elevation gains, particularly within Els Ports Natural Park, featuring stony tracks and winding curves.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails near Tortosa?
A: Yes, the region offers excellent options for families. The &lt;strong&gt;Via Verda (Greenway) de Val de Zafan&lt;/strong&gt; is particularly suitable, as it's an old railway track converted into a car-free path. The section from Tortosa to Xerta, known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946137928/via-verde-de-tortosa-a-xerta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Tortosa a Xerta&lt;/a&gt;, is an easy 17.4-mile (28.0 km) trail perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for the Ebro Delta?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;strong&gt;Ebro Delta Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers a distinct mountain biking experience with its flat, expansive landscapes. Routes like the "Rice Land" route, which follows the old Ebro Natural Trail, provide a unique opportunity to cycle through wetlands and observe rich birdlife, including flamingos. These trails are generally less strenuous than those in the mountainous areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails near Tortosa?
A: The trails offer stunning natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the rugged terrain of &lt;strong&gt;Els Ports Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, with its ravines and dense forests, or the unique wetland ecosystems of the &lt;strong&gt;Ebro Delta&lt;/strong&gt;. Along the Ebro River, you'll encounter scenic views and historic structures like the old red railway bridge. Notable highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4959065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pool&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullals de Baltasar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike trails available around Tortosa?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31564579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ebro River – Old railway viaduct loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.6-mile (49.2 km) path offering scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5317969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tortosa Municipal Market – Argilers loop from Tortosa&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.5-mile (41.1 km) trail through varied terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Tortosa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to relaxed riverbank rides, and the stunning natural beauty of both Els Ports Natural Park and the Ebro Delta. The well-maintained greenways and variety of routes for all skill levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes with significant elevation gain for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for advanced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with substantial elevation gain, particularly within &lt;strong&gt;Els Ports Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. Trails leading to areas like the hermitage of Mig Camí, up to the chapel of Coll de l'Alba, or into the heart of Els Ports Natural Park itself can feature climbs of up to 20% and demanding ascents across areas like Vall Cervera.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tortosa?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Tortosa, with milder temperatures and less rainfall. Summers can be very hot, especially in the Ebro Delta, while winters are typically mild but can bring cooler, wetter weather to the mountainous areas. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any huts or refuges available along the mountain bike trails for longer rides?
A: Yes, particularly in the mountainous areas like Els Ports Natural Park, there are several huts and refuges that can be useful for longer rides or multi-day adventures. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/786423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugi Caro UEC&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5092818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Forestal de la Tenalla&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4980710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Ferrera Refuge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in total around Tortosa?
A: There are over 100 mountain bike trails around Tortosa listed on komoot, offering a wide variety of experiences. This includes 9 easy routes, 63 moderate routes, and 29 difficult routes, ensuring options for every skill level and preference.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails around Tortosa using public transport?
A: While direct public transport to every trailhead might be limited, Tortosa itself is accessible by public transport. Some routes, particularly those starting directly from Tortosa or along the Via Verda, might be more easily reached. For trails deeper in Els Ports Natural Park, a car or local taxi service might be necessary to reach the starting points.
tips: Church with two naves located in Xerta. The building stands out for the white color of the main façade and a tower, in the neo-Romanesque style culminated by a modernist-style wrought iron lightning rod.
The jetty serves as a viewpoint with views of the island located in the middle of the Ebro river as it passes through Xerta, a municipality that is located in a pronounced meander.
Former railway bridge over the Ebro and direct entry into Tortosa. Excellent panoramic view of the river and the city.
